channel.title: New local problems on FixMyStreet
channel.description: The latest local problems reported by users

item.title: More rubbish from someone's flat renovation, 9th March
item.description:
More rubbish that has been here all weekend. Just dumped at the entrance to the building. Surely it would be possible to work out who is responsible?

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Bakers Hill, Hackney, London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Grass on Belmont Open Space, 9th March
item.description:
The grass on Belmont Open Space needs cutting please to enable the fitness classes - for which the instructor has an LBB licence - to run her classes. Please can this be done asap? Thank you
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 10,12, Imperial Way, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: V large area of graffiti, 9th March
item.description:
On side wall of Hackney Central bus garage.

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Mare Street, London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Can't read street sign, 9th March
item.description:
'Dukes Orchard ' street name sign has faded badly and is hardly readable.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Dukes Orchard, Coldblow, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Another tree vandalised with grafif, 9th March
item.description:
Tree in centre of square field has been graffiti-ed. This in the second time this has happened.

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Mare Street, London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Caravan full of rubbish dumped on the road, 9th March
item.description:
Again a caravan insecure and full of rubbish has been dumped outside the travellers site. it is blocking one lane as well as the cycle lane.
Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Thistlebrook, Abbey Wood
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Fencing Council workmen have left, 9th March
item.description:
Fencing Council workmen have left outside my property it's been there since the 20th of February
Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Broad Walk, Kidbrooke
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Tree roots damage to pavement, 9th March
item.description:
The roots from the tree outside 4 sylvester avenue are lifting the pavement and also pretuding into the property this has damaged the driveway and also will potentially damage the brick wall surrounding the property the tree looks in a very poor state and I wonder if it may be dead there are small branches falling off onto the pavement which is obviously dangerous

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Sylvester Avenue, Elmstead, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Outside 2 Hartland Road, 9th March
item.description:
Discarded rubbish, food containers. As usual street a mess.

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Honiton Road, London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Human faeces in playground, 9th March
item.description:
Camberwell Green playground has human poo under the climbing frame

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Camberwell Green, Camberwell, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Outside 22 Hartland road NW6, 9th March
item.description:
A table and two chairs dumped on pavement.

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Hartland Road, London
Report on FixMyStreet
item.category: Fly-tip Medium - Up to medium size van load

item.title: Broken rail, 9th March
item.description:
Broken post and rail fence

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Kingsground, Eltham
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Sinkhole under pavement, 9th March
item.description:
Sinkhole opened under drive that extends to under the pavement, is concerned this will giveway due to high traffic JW
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 55, Chelsfield Road, Poverest, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Rubbish and large items not being removed, 9th March
item.description:
I made the initial report on 6th of March and I received an email on the 7th of March stating that the issue has been “fixed” but today is the 9th and the picture attached was taken this afternoon so someone is LYING. Please escalate this issue and forward the email or phone number with someone I can speak to directly about this issue and file a complaint. I don’t work for Southwark this is not my job, but this is where I live and I don’t appreciate how you as the council who takes mine and my neighbours money for certain services but are not actually providing them.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Wivenhoe Close, Peckham, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Another barrier removed, 9th March
item.description:
Another barrier removed. This is in addition to the one first reported in September 2025

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Kingsground, Eltham
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Urgent Request for Road Safety Measures – St James Road/Rotherhithe New Rd and Surrounding Area, 9th March
item.description:
I am writing to raise serious concerns regarding road safety in the St James Road area, particularly around the nearby schools. There are at least four schools in this area, and I have personally observed numerous near misses and several collisions involving pedestrians, including children. To improve safety, I respectfully request that the council take the following actions without delay: Introduce additional speed calming measures throughout the area. Install improved school signage, including flashing lights during school hours. Repaint faded road markings to ensure visibility. Consider additional road humps, particularly for vehicles entering St James Road from Old Kent Road, outside Selco. Introduce further road calming measures at the junction of St James Road and Rotherhithe New Road, which is a particularly dangerous location. Install another pedestrian crossing at the entrance to Selco’s car park. These measures are urgently required to prevent further accidents and to protect the safety of pedestrians, particularly school children.
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): St James's Road, Old Kent Road, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Pavement has lifted, 9th March
item.description:
The pavement walkway has left and caused a child to fall off there scooter
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Ravenscroft Crescent, Mottingham, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Doug Fouling, 9th March
item.description:
Dog waste along pavement outside of 52 Walsingham Road. Please clear.
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 49,51, Walsingham Road, St Paul's Cray, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Speeding vehicles - danger to school children, 9th March
item.description:
I am writing to raise serious concerns regarding road safety in the St James Road area, particularly around the nearby schools. There are at least four schools in this area, and I have personally observed numerous near misses and several collisions involving pedestrians, including children. To improve safety, I respectfully request that the council take the following actions without delay: Introduce additional speed calming measures throughout the area. Install improved school signage, including flashing lights during school hours. Repaint faded road markings to ensure visibility. Consider additional road humps, particularly for vehicles entering St James Road from Old Kent Road, outside Selco. Introduce further road calming measures at the junction of St James Road and Rotherhithe New Road, which is a particularly dangerous location. Install another pedestrian crossing at the entrance to Selco’s car park. These measures are urgently required to prevent further accidents and to protect the safety of pedestrians, particularly school children.
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): St James's Road, Old Kent Road, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Box dumped at the end of Studland Road, 9th March
item.description:
Box in picture has been dumped at the end of the road by park railing. Reason for report as contents are metal nitrous oxide bottles which could be potentially dangerous

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Studland Road, Penge, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet
